rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOE VS. KINK.COM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Witness Angel Anders Answers To Deposition Questions And Further Deposition As To All Related Follow-Up Questions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 16, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JOHN DOE'S Motion To Compel Witness Angel Anders Answers To Deposition Questions And Further Deposition As To All Related Follow-Up Questions. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. The parties should be prepared to address the scope of H & S Code §120975As noted by Justice Moreno in his dissent in John B. (2006) 38 Cal.4th 1177, 1218 the scope of §120975 was not addressed in John B and has not specifically been addressed by any other California appellate court. Plaintiff claims it is limited to disclosure of mandatory donors of blood, Defendants, like Justice Moreno, read it more broadly. (Id. at 1218-1219.) In Irwin the court of appeal vacated an order which attempted to balance the privacy rights of a donor with the need for information in litigation against a blood bank by holding that §120975 was a complete bar. If §120975 applies to the instant discovery, the motion would be denied. If it does not, then the court would have to engage in a balancing test. (In re Clergy Cases I (2010) 188 Cal.App.4th 1224, 1235.) There is no question that the information sought is highly sensitive. The parties should be prepared to address the balancing test. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM VS. FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response To Request For Production Of Documents Set Three To Martin Mcnerney Development Inc Dba Martin Building Company
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 16, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM'S Motion To Compel Further Response To Request For Production Of Documents Set Three To Martin Mcnerney Development Inc Dba Martin Building Company. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel is granted. Defendant shall file a supplemental verified respond on or before August 31, 2018 indicating that the documents requested have been produced to the depository and if any documents are withheld or redacted, a privilege log shall be provided. To the extent that the documents have already been produced or do not exist, Defendant shall provide that information in the response. Assuming that Defendant complies with this order, any issues regarding privilege determinations shall be addressed by the Special Master so as to allow Plaintiff to complete any additional discovery based on these documents before the November trial date. With regard to sanctions, Plaintiff should have first sought resolution of this issue with the Special Master. Defendant, however, caused this motion to be filed by failing to provide any reasonable options to Plaintiff in light of the upcoming trial date and despite being on notice since January of 2018 that such documents would likely be at issue in light of the alter ego claim. As a result sanctions are reduced to $3260. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN EXPRESS BANK, FSB VS. WILLIAM M PEREZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Entering Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 16, 2018, Line 3, PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS BANK, FSB's Motion For Order Entering Judgment. Plaintiff American Express Bank, FSB's motion for order entering judgment is granted in part. No opposition filed. American Express Bank is entitled to a judgment in its favor and against defendant William Perez in the amount of $9,630, inclusive of all costs including all costs claimed on its memorandum of costs filed June 28, 2018. Per the parties' stipulation and Ms. Dubowski's declaration, American Express Bank is entitled to the settlement amount of $12,600 less payments of $3,030 toward the settlement amount plus costs of $60 claimed on its June 28, 2018 memorandum of costs that were not already included in the settlement amount. Counsel for American Express Bank is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a proposed judgment that conforms to the tentative ruling and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: JESSICA TAYLOR VS. MARIADB USA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Mariadb Usa, Inc. Motion For Summary Judgment Mariadb Usa, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication (For Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 16, 2018, Line 4, Defendant Mariadb Usa, Inc.'s Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication (part 2 of 2)(For Tentative Ruling Only) That evidence included: (1) Ms. Lochead removed Ms. Taylor from the project after Ms. Taylor's protected disclosure; (2) no investigation was made after Ms. Taylor complained of retaliatory treatment to Mr. Larsen; (3) there is no record of performance issues prior to Ms. Taylor's protected activity; (4) the only record of unprofessional behavior is the email exchange in which Ms. Taylor requested a copy of the emails she suspected were in violation of the law; (5) MariaDB presented shifting and inconsistent explanations for terminating Ms. Taylor's employment; and (6) a substantially similar position was available soon after the termination of Ms. Taylor's employment. There is a triable dispute whether Ms. Taylor was paid her wages in full precluding summary adjudication on the failure to pay wages claim. MariaDB did not satisfy its summary adjudication burden of showing that it did not willfully fail to pay wages owed immediately upon the termination of Ms. Taylor's employment precluding summary adjudication on the waiting time penalties claim. MariaDB is entitled to summary adjudication on the failure to pay expenses claim based on the undisputed facts that Ms. Taylor was given the opportunity to purchase the company laptop and she did not return it, more than offsetting any amount MariaDB owed her for business expenses. The email preceding the offer letter and the confidentiality agreement is not a part of Ms. Taylor's employment contract and does survive the contract per the contract's integration clause. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Taylor is required to prepare a proposed order which complies with CCP 437c(g) and repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.(end of part 2 of 2)=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: JESSICA TAYLOR VS. MARIADB USA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Mariadb Usa, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 16, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT MARIADB USA, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.(part 1 of 2 of tentative ruling) Defendant MariaDB USA, Inc's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the fifth cause for violation of Labor Code 2802(a) and is denied as to the four other causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff Jessica Taylor. Using a multi-faceted approach, the court concludes that the California Labor Code provisions that Ms. Tayor alleges were violated by MariaDB are applicable to this case even though the alleged violations occurred outside California. The most pertinent facts supporting the application of the Labor Code provisions are: Ms. Taylor contracted for employment in California with an employer whose North America nerve center is in California; at all times while she worked for MariaDB, Ms. Taylor was a California resident per federal law; the parties' contract specified that Ms. Taylor's primary work location would be her home and included a California choice of law provision; Ms. Taylor worked approximately one quarter of her employment with MariaDB in California; Ms. Taylor's relocation to Florida was temporary, occasioned by her spouse's active military duty, subsequent to which she returned to California; Ms. Taylor's employment was terminated during her temporary stay in Florida; and the decision to terminate Ms. Taylor's employment was made by persons who were not located in either California or Florida. These facts strongly suggest that California's strong public policy of protecting its workers would be undermined if Ms. Taylor lost the protections of the California Labor Code because her employment terminated when she had temporarily moved to Florida to accompany her husband who was on active military duty. There are triable disputes as to Ms. Taylor's Labor Code 1102.5 claims. Section 1102.5 cannot reasonably be construed to exclude protection of an employee who is subjected to an adverse employment action because she protested an impending violation of the law on the ground that the employer decided not to proceed with the illegal conduct. Carter v. Escondido Union High School Dist. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 922 is distinguishable. In Carter the plaintiff's disclosure was not motivated by the perceived illegality of the act and the act itself could not be reasonably believed to be illegal. While temporal proximity alone is insufficient to create a triable issue of pretext, Ms. Taylor submitted sufficient specific and substantial evidence for a reasonable juror to find that her protected activity was a substantial motivating factor for the termination of her employment. (end of part 1 of 2)=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: SUSANNA CHEUNG VS. MARK YIU KONG CHENG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 16, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT GOLDEN AGE VINTAGE's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Hearing required. A threshold issue not discussed by either side is what type of business entity is Golden Age Vintage. If Golden Age Vintage is merely a tradename or DBA for defendants Mark and/or Florence Cheng, then there can be no separate liability against it and there is no need for Golden Age Vintage to file a separate answer. If Golden Age Vintage is a legally separate business entity from the Chengs, then the Chengs, as non-lawyers, may not represent Golden Age Vintage including seeking to set aside the May 3, 2018 default entered against it.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: GUADALUPE HERNANDEZ GOMEZ VS. ANDRIGHETTO PRODUCE INC. A CALIFORNIA PRODUCE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 16, 2018, Line 8, DEFENDANT ANDRIGHETTO PRODUCE INC., D/B/A SHASTA PRODUCE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Andrighetto Produce Incorporated's demurrer to the fourth through tenth causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Guadalupe Hernandez Gomez is sustained without leave to amend as to the ninth cause of action and overruled as to all other causes of action. Liberally construed, the fourth through eighth and tenth causes of action all contain sufficient allegations of ultimate facts supporting Ms. Gomez's claims for violations of FEHA and CFRA, discrimination, failure to accommodate, retaliation and wrongful termination. The fourth, sixth and seventh causes of action adequately allege that Ms. Gomez was qualified to do her job and her employment was terminated because of her pregnancy, pregnancy-related disability and gender. The fifth cause of action adequately alleges that Ms. Gomez was qualified to do her job and she requested reasonable accommodations which were ignored or denied. The eighth cause of action adequately alleges that Ms. Gomez was qualified to do her job and that her employment was terminated because she requested pregnancy-related medical leave. The tenth cause of action adequately alleges that Ms. Gomez was qualified to do her job and her employment was terminated because she requested reasonable accommodations. The demurrer to the fourth through eighth and tenth causes of action appear to be based on a misapprehension that Ms. Gomez is required to allege evidentiary, rather than ultimate, facts to withstand a pleading challenge. The ninth cause of action is entirely duplicative of the fifth cause of action and Ms. Gomez fails to explain why she needs two identical claims entitled "Failure to Accommodate" based on the same alleged facts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant American Arbitration Association'S Demurrer To The First Amended Complaint. (Per Ex Parte Order Filed 8/13/18)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 16, 2018, Line 10, Defendant American Arbitration Association'S Demurrer To The First Amended Complaint. (Per Ex Parte Order Filed 8/13/18). Defendant American Arbitration Association's demurrer to the fourth and fifth causes of action in the first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to both causes of action. AAA's request for judicial notice of the Escrow Agreement, the Partial Final Award, and Procedural Order No. 11 is granted as to all three documents. Based on those documents, the fourth and fifth causes of action fail as a matter of law since those documents establish that AAA did not breach any duty to plaintiffs J Garcia Carrion, SA and CIV USA, Inc. when AAA disbursed the funds it held per the Escrow Agreement to defendants per the directions of the arbitrators in the Partial Final Award. As a matter of law, once the Partial Final Award was issued, AAA had no obligation to retain the funds it held per the Escrow Agreement pending an agreement by the parties to the arbitration as to the disposition of those funds. Plaintiffs' request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibits A, B, and F and denied as to the remaining exhibits. As to the exhibits which the court has not taken judicial notice, the court has considered the contents of those exhibits as a proffer of what plaintiffs would allege in an amended complaint if they were granted leave to amend. Exhibits C-E and G-J do not show that plaintiffs are able to allege any viable claims against AAA because all of those exhibits pre-date, and as a matter of law any offers or admissions therein are superseded by, the Partial Final Award. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for AAA is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: J GARCIA CARRION, SA, A CORPORATION ORGANIZED AND ET AL VS. AMERICAN ARBITRATION ASSOCIATION INC. A NEW YORK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 16, 2018, Line 10, DEFENDANT VINCENT FRIEND, CORDELINA WINE COMPANY, FRIEND WINE's MARKETING, INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Hearing required. The court re-affirms the tentative ruling issued prior to the August 1 hearing and provides additional language regarding plaintiffs' request for judicial notice: Defendants Vincent Friend, Cordelina Wine Company and Friend Wine Marketing, Inc.'s demurrer to the first through third and sixth through eleventh causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiffs J Garcia Carrion, SA and CIV USA, Inc. is sustained without leave to amend as to all causes of action. The doctrine of claim preclusion bars all of plaintiffs' claims premised on their alleged ownership of FWM stock because the ownership of the stock was an issue "within the scope of the [arbitral] action, related to the subject-matter and relevant to the issues, so that it could have been raised, the judgment is conclusive on it despite the fact that it was not in fact expressly pleaded or otherwise urged." (Bucur v. Ahmad (2016) 244 Cal.App.4th 175, 185). Because the third and sixth through eleventh causes of action are all premised on plaintiffs' ownership of the FWM, all of those claims fail. The first and second causes of action for breach of the escrow agreement and the implied good faith covenant therein are also barred by the doctrine of claim preclusion. Those claims are based on the allegation that the manner and timing of the distribution of the money held in escrow to defendants violated plaintiffs' contract rights. However, whether, when and how the money held in escrow should be distributed to defendants was or could have been litigated in the arbitration proceedings and in the proceedings before this court on the motion to confirm the arbitration award. The doctrine of claim preclusion now bars plaintiffs' claims raising those issues outside the arbitration or a post-arbitration motion to confirm, correct or vacate the arbitration award. Plaintiffs' request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibits A, B, and F and denied as to the remaining exhibits. As to the exhibits which the court has not taken judicial notice, the court has considered the contents of those exhibits as a proffer of what plaintiffs would allege in an amended complaint if they were granted leave to amend. Exhibits C-E and G-J do not show that plaintiffs are able to allege any viable claims against demurring defendants because all of those exhibits pre-date, and as a matter of law any offers or admissions therein are superseded by, the Partial Final Award. Counsel for the demurring defendants is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats the tentative ruling verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: MUZHAN SHAHRESTANI VS. ANTONY A. RILEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 16, 2018, Line 11, DEFENDANT ANTONY RILEY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Antony Riley's demurrer to the first cause of action for breach of contract in the complaint filed by plaintiff Muzhan Shahrestani is overruled. Per well-settled demurrer and breach of contract rules, the court's task on this demurrer is to determine whether, in light of the asserted extrinsic evidence, the guaranty agreement is reasonably susceptible to Ms. Shahrestani's proffered interpretations of that agreement. It is. Liberally construing the allegations in the complaint and considering all of those allegations and the documents attached to the complaint, the guaranty agreement is reasonably susceptible to both of the following interpretations: 1) the guaranty agreement covers the initial investment made by Ms. Shahrestani as reflected in the shareholder agreement and 2) the guaranty agreement covers the entirety of the investment made by Ms. Shahrestani up to but not exceeding $170,000. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Shahrestani is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)
